title: Association
description: Schema for association JSON files that define relationships between classes. The same schema is used at three directory levels, each with different class key scoping rules. Subdomain level (subdomains/{s}/class_associations/): both classes are in the same subdomain, use bare class keys like 'order'. Domain level (domains/{d}/class_associations/): classes are in different subdomains of the same domain, use 'subdomain/class' keys like 'checkout/order'. Model level (class_associations/): classes are in different domains, use 'domain/subdomain/class' keys like 'commerce/checkout/order'. The association filename encodes the two class keys and a name: '{from}--{to}--{name}.assoc.json'. Associations connect two classes and define the cardinality (multiplicity) of the relationship. If validation fails, verify all required fields are present (name, from_class_key, from_multiplicity, to_class_key, to_multiplicity) and that class keys reference existing classes using the correct scoping format for the directory level.

name.description: The display name of the association (e.g., 'Order Contains Items', 'Customer Places Order'). This is the human-readable name shown in diagrams. Use verb phrases that describe the relationship. Must be non-empty. If validation fails: ensure this is a string with at least one character.

details.description: Optional detailed description of the association. Describe the business meaning of this relationship, any constraints, and how the connected classes interact. Helps readers understand why these classes are related.

from_class_key.description: Key referencing the source (from) class in the association. The format depends on the directory level: in a subdomain's class_associations/ use just the class key (e.g. 'order'); in a domain's class_associations/ use 'subdomain/class' (e.g. 'checkout/order'); in the model root class_associations/ use 'domain/subdomain/class' (e.g. 'commerce/checkout/order'). The key must match a class that exists in the model. If validation fails: check the scoping format matches the directory level and that the referenced class exists.

from_multiplicity.description: Multiplicity at the source (from) end of the association. Format is either a single number (e.g., '1', '2') or a range 'lower..upper'. Use '*' for unbounded (converted internally to 'any' or 'many'). Common values: '1' (exactly one), '0..1' (zero or one), '*' or 'any' (zero or more), '1..*' or '1..many' (one or more), or specific ranges like '2..5'. The upper bound must be >= the lower bound. This indicates how many instances of the from_class can be associated with one instance of the to_class. If validation fails: ensure this follows the format 'N', 'N..M', 'N..*', or 'N..many' where N and M are non-negative integers.

to_class_key.description: Key referencing the target (to) class in the association. The format depends on the directory level: in a subdomain's class_associations/ use just the class key (e.g. 'line_item'); in a domain's class_associations/ use 'subdomain/class' (e.g. 'shipping/shipment'); in the model root class_associations/ use 'domain/subdomain/class' (e.g. 'logistics/shipping/shipment'). The key must match a class that exists in the model. If validation fails: check the scoping format matches the directory level and that the referenced class exists.

to_multiplicity.description: Multiplicity at the target (to) end of the association. Format is either a single number (e.g., '1', '2') or a range 'lower..upper'. Use '*' for unbounded (converted internally to 'any' or 'many'). Common values: '1' (exactly one), '0..1' (zero or one), '*' or 'any' (zero or more), '1..*' or '1..many' (one or more), or specific ranges like '2..5'. The upper bound must be >= the lower bound. This indicates how many instances of the to_class can be associated with one instance of the from_class. If validation fails: ensure this follows the format 'N', 'N..M', 'N..*', or 'N..many' where N and M are non-negative integers.

association_class_key.description: Optional key referencing an association class that holds attributes about the relationship itself. For example, an 'Enrollment' class between 'Student' and 'Course' might hold 'grade' and 'enrollment_date'. The association class must exist in the same scope as the association. Use the same scoping format as from_class_key and to_class_key. Set to null or omit if there is no association class. If validation fails: verify the association class file exists and the key uses the correct scoping format for this directory level.

uml_comment.description: Optional UML comment for diagram annotations. This text appears as a note attached to the association line in generated UML diagrams. Use for diagram-specific clarifications.
